examples.name: Example 1: Single-Environment Analysis (Minimal)
examples.description: Simplest valid single-environment request with 2 genotypes, 3 reps each

expected_response_structure.interpretation: [Publication-ready text with heritability classification and recommendations]

examples.name: Example 2: Multi-Environment Analysis (Fixed Model)
examples.description: Multi-environment request with 3 genotypes, 2 environments, 2 reps per G×E

expected_response_structure.interpretation: [Text explaining G×E interaction significance and environment-specific selection strategies]

examples.name: Example 3: Multi-Environment Analysis (Random Model)
examples.description: Same as Example 2 but treating environment as random effect (includes σ²E in h² denominator)
examples.note: With random_environment=true, the h² denominator includes environmental variance component, resulting in lower h² than fixed model. Use this when environments are considered a random sample from a population of possible environments.

examples.name: Example 4: Edge Case - Minimal Data (Negative Variance Warning)
examples.description: Small dataset that may trigger negative variance warning: 3 genotypes, 2 reps

low_heritability.message: Heritability is very low (<10%). Genetic control of this trait is weak.
low_heritability.implication: Selection response will be minimal. Environmental variation dominates.

expected_response_structure.interpretation: [WARNING section highlighting weak genetic signal and recommendation to focus on environmental management]

examples.name: Example 5: Data Validation Endpoint
examples.description: Pre-flight data validation without running full analysis

examples.name: Example 6: Invalid Request - Missing Environment Column
Example 7: Invalid Request - Insufficient Data
examples.description: Request with fewer than 6 observations (minimum required)

errors.validation: data must contain at least 6 observations

examples.name: Example 8: Large Dataset - 50 Genotypes, Single Environment
examples.description: Real-world scale dataset: 50 genotypes, 4 replicates = 200 observations

examples.name: Example 9: Large Multi-Environment - 20 Genotypes, 5 Environments
examples.description: Large multi-environment trial: 20 G × 5 E × 3 reps = 300 observations

examples.name: Example 10: Using API from R Client
examples.description: How to call VivaSense API from R using httr or curl
examples.r_code:
library(httr)
library(jsonlite)

# Prepare data
data <- data.frame(
  genotype = rep(c('G01', 'G02'), each = 3),
  rep = rep(c('R1', 'R2', 'R3'), 2),
  trait_value = c(45.2, 46.1, 44.8, 48.5, 49.2, 48.9)
)

# Make request
response <- POST(
  url = 'http://localhost:8000/genetics/analyze',
  body = toJSON(list(
    data = data,
    mode = 'single',
    trait_name = 'Plant Height (cm)'
  )),
  content_type_json()
)

# Parse response
result <- fromJSON(content(response, as = 'text'))
print(result$interpretation)

examples.name: Example 11: Using API from Python Client
examples.description: How to call VivaSense API from Python using requests
examples.python_code:
import requests
import json

# Prepare data
data = [
    {'genotype': 'G01', 'rep': 'R1', 'trait_value': 45.2},
    {'genotype': 'G01', 'rep': 'R2', 'trait_value': 46.1},
    {'genotype': 'G01', 'rep': 'R3', 'trait_value': 44.8},
    {'genotype': 'G02', 'rep': 'R1', 'trait_value': 48.5},
    {'genotype': 'G02', 'rep': 'R2', 'trait_value': 49.2},
    {'genotype': 'G02', 'rep': 'R3', 'trait_value': 48.9}
]

# Make request
response = requests.post(
    url='http://localhost:8000/genetics/analyze',
    json={
        'data': data,
        'mode': 'single',
        'trait_name': 'Plant Height (cm)'
    }
)

# Parse response
result = response.json()
print(result['interpretation'])
print(f"h² = {result['result']['heritability']['h2_broad_sense']}")

h2_ranges.0.0_to_0.1: Very low genetic control - environmental factors dominate
h2_ranges.0.3_to_0.5: Moderate heritability - both genetic and environmental effects important
h2_ranges.0.5_to_0.7: Moderately high heritability - genetic basis strong
h2_ranges.0.7_to_1.0: High heritability - excellent genetic basis for selection

gcv_pcv_interpretation.GCV_PCV_ratio_high: Genetic variation is substantial relative to environmental variation
gcv_pcv_interpretation.GCV_PCV_ratio_low: Environmental variation is high relative to genetic variation

ge_interaction.sigma2_ge_high: Genotype ranks change across environments - consider environment-specific breeding
ge_interaction.sigma2_ge_low: Genotype ranks stable - single-location selection effective
